Title: Functional specialization of UDP-glycosyltransferase 73P12 in licorice to produce a sweet triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-glycosyltransferase; PSPG motif-containing UGT73P subfamily
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: canonical from glycyrrhizin-producing strain; natural variant from glycyrrhizin-deficient strain 83-555
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Sweetener and important active ingredient in traditional herbal medicine; reduced side-effects compared with aglycone
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stolons (underground organs, especially thickened main roots)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Terminal glycosylation branch of oleanane-type triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a glucuronosyl moiety from UDP-glucuronic acid to glycyrrhetinic acid 3-O-monoglucuronide to form glycyrrhizin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Final-step enzyme; key terminal determinant supported, rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots of high-glycyrrhizin-producing strain 308-19 harvested in June; expressed exclusively in roots together with bAS, CYP88D6 and CYP72A154; low/absent in leaves and reduced in dormant season/low-producing strain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Underground organs/roots; correlated with glycyrrhizin accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seasonal/developmental state of aerial growth influences expression/productivity; compared June active growth versus December dormancy; high- versus low-producing strains
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome coexpression mining; phylogenetic analysis; recombinant protein expression in Sf9 and E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays with authentic substrates; LC-MS product identification; catalytic residue mutagenesis (H29A, D131A); natural variant comparison; residue-swapping mutagenesis (R32S/S32R); kinetic analysis; UDP-sugar competition assays; homology modeling and docking
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Canonical UGT73P12 promotes glycyrrhizin production, whereas the natural 83-555 variant loses UDP-glucuronic acid specificity and predominantly produces glucoglycyrrhizin instead
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73P12 was identified from licorice transcriptome data as a root-expressed UGT coexpressed with known glycyrrhizin biosynthetic genes. 2. Recombinant canonical UGT73P12 catalyzed the second glucuronosylation of glycyrrhetinic acid 3-O-monoglucuronide to produce glycyrrhizin, but did not use glycyrrhetinic acid directly. 3. The enzyme showed strong preference for UDP-glucuronic acid over other UDP-sugars. 4. A natural UGT73P12 variant from glycyrrhizin-deficient strain 83-555 shifted donor specificity and produced glucoglycyrrhizin, matching the plant chemotype. 5. Arg32 was demonstrated by modeling and residue swapping to be essential for high UDP-glucuronic acid specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome analysis of four G. uralensis cDNA libraries (Ramilowski et al., 2013); no database accession reported in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic essential residues His29 and Asp131 were required for activity. Canonical enzyme showed no activity toward glycyrrhetinic acid, implying another enzyme performs the first glucuronosylation. Natural variant differs by 8 amino acids; Arg32 is decisive for donor specificity but not apparent catalytic efficiency under competition conditions. Variant product is glucoglycyrrhizin.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful terminal-step gene for engineering glycyrrhizin biosynthesis in heterologous hosts or licorice improvement. Arg32-guided engineering can reprogram UDP-sugar donor specificity to design novel sweet triterpenoid saponins.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Variant or engineered loss of UDP-glucuronic acid specificity redirects product to glucoglycyrrhizin; no growth/yield penalty reported

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Family 1 plant UDP-glycosyltransferase; UGT73P subfamily; PSPG motif-containing
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Soyasaponin branch of oleanane-type triterpenoid saponin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a galactosyl moiety from UDP-galactose to soyasapogenol B 3-O-monoglucuronide to produce soyasaponin III
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression correlated with CYP93E3 and UGT91H11 in transcriptome data
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Likely root-associated based on coexpression cluster, but explicit tissue detail limited
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; recombinant protein assay; LC-MS; comparison of UDP-sugar selectivity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces soyasaponin III via UDP-galactose-dependent galactosylation; lacks UDP-glucuronic acid specificity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT73P13 is closely related to soybean GmSGT2. 2. It was experimentally shown to transfer galactose from UDP-galactose to soyasapogenol B 3-O-monoglucuronide to produce soyasaponin III. 3. It represents a paralog of UGT73P12 with different UDP-sugar specificity, highlighting functional divergence in the UGT73P subfamily.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq transcriptome analysis of four G. uralensis cDNA libraries; no accession reported in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Analyzed mainly to confirm UDP-galactose selectivity and as a paralogous contrast to UGT73P12; Pro25 replaces the arginine-equivalent position linked to UDP-glucuronic acid specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential gene for engineering soyasaponin sugar diversification and comparative UGT specificity studies
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May channel flux to soyasaponin branch rather than glycyrrhizin branch
